name: Angel Aligner — Orthodontics (New Logo)
contact_strategy: Prefer a named treatment coordinator or lead hygienist when one appears on the site — they see patient satisfaction, struggling cases, and scanner reliability in real time, and workflow friction beats brand loyalty in their purchase influence. The reason phrase should reference what makes their job easier (case workflow, scanning, patient experience). If neither role is named, prefer the practice owner or lead orthodontist; fall back to the first listed provider.
description: Independent orthodontic practices in Sun Belt growth metros that actively offer clear aligner treatment (non-Invisalign brand confirmed on site), have adopted digital scanning workflows, and market to adult and teen patients. The ideal target has a confirmed digital-workflow foundation (intraoral scanner named on site), visible patient financing, and direct budget authority (independent ownership). High-tier Invisalign practices (Diamond+) that exclusively list Invisalign are EXCLUDED — they are commercially unreachable (volume lock-in, iTero dependency, no multi-brand evidence). High-tier practices that have also opened their aligner stack (ClearCorrect, Spark, or SureSmile on site) route to Contender for a lab-fee delta pitch. DSO-affiliated practices route to Contender for corporate procurement. Hospital-employed, academic, and general-dentist-only practices are excluded. Angel Aligner already-providers are excluded (new-logo campaign only).
scoring_notes: Clear aligner adoption is the primary gate — required_for_bullseye ensures no Bullseye without a confirmed non-Invisalign aligner offering (S-OA-001). An Invisalign-only practice does not satisfy this gate. Four scored signals (clear_aligners_non_invisalign, open_intraoral_scanner, patient_financing, treats_adults) represent the clinical and commercial readiness profile; independent_practice is the wedge. The scanner signal counts only brand-agnostic scanners (Trios, Medit, Primescan, Carestream) — iTero is Align's proprietary scanner and scores as friction (-15): an iTero-only practice has its digital workflow built around Invisalign. Competing-brand listings (Spark, ClearCorrect, SureSmile) reinforce the open-scanner signal, since those brands run on open STL workflows. Zero-weight contextual signals are captured by the LLM for sales angle and call brief quality but do not affect fit_signal_score. Recommended bullseye_min_score: 90 (default). The four core signals at high confidence produce a fit score of ~91. S-OA-006 (invisalign_high_tier_exclusive) is a HARD EXCLUSION: high-tier badge + Invisalign-only = commercially unreachable, do not call. S-OA-020 is the routing flag when a high-tier practice also lists competing brands — capped at Contender for a lab-fee delta pitch. S-OA-007 (dso_corporate_buyer) caps at Contender for corporate procurement. None of these routing signals alter fit_signal_score.

hypothesis.target_profile: Independent orthodontist in a Sun Belt metro who already offers clear aligners and has adopted intraoral scanning — this practice understands the digital workflow and is actively competing on aligner outcomes. The gap is margin: Invisalign lab fees run $1,000–$1,500 per case above comparable Angel outcomes. An orthodontist doing 200 aligner cases per year is leaving $200K–$300K on the table annually. Angel's pitch is not 'switch away from Invisalign' — it is 'use Angel for cost-sensitive patients and first-consult capture so you stop losing cases to price.'
hypothesis.wedge: Lab-fee delta on comparable outcomes. Roughly $1,000 savings per case vs. Invisalign Comprehensive. For a practice doing volume, this is a practice-economics argument, not a product feature argument.
hypothesis.friction: High-tier Invisalign badge (Diamond+) indicates deep Align relationship and possible volume incentive lock-in. DSO practices require corporate procurement approval. Both route to Contender with adjusted sales angle.
hypothesis.common_objections:
Q: We're already Diamond with Invisalign, why would we switch?
A: Angel is not a replacement — it is a parallel offering for the cost-sensitive consult you are currently losing or discounting. Your Invisalign volume protects your badge tier; Angel handles the cases where the Invisalign fee is the objection.

Q: Our patients ask for Invisalign by name.
A: Patient brand awareness is real. Angel's play is staff-level recommendation for financing-sensitive or self-pay patients — the patient who got a quote elsewhere and didn't proceed. You convert cases you are currently not closing.

Q: Our current lab turnaround is fine.
A: Turnaround is not the differentiator. The argument is per-case economics. On a 200-case year, the lab-fee delta alone is significant practice revenue.

source_urls.nppes_taxonomy: https://taxonomy.nucc.org/ — NPPES code 1223X0400X confirms Orthodontics specialty
source_urls.invisalign_doctor_locator: https://www.invisalign.com/find-a-doctor — observable badge tiers (Diamond, Diamond Plus, Top 1%)
source_urls.angel_aligner_us: https://www.angelaligner.com/en-us/ — product line reference (Angel Pro, Angel KiD, A6)

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice explicitly list clear aligner treatment as a service, using a brand OTHER than Invisalign — such as ClearCorrect, Spark, SureSmile, Angel Aligner, or similar? Do NOT mark 'yes' if Invisalign is the only aligner brand mentioned.
signals.note: Primary clinical-fit gate. A 'yes' requires at least one non-Invisalign aligner brand listed — Invisalign-only practices do not satisfy this gate because they are either deep Align partners (captured by S-OA-006) or potential prospects who still need to add a non-Invisalign option. Confirmed 'no' caps at Contender; not_found caps at Needs Verification.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice's website name a brand-agnostic intraoral scanner — 3Shape Trios, Medit, Primescan, Carestream, or a similar open-STL scanner — as equipment they use? Do NOT count iTero; iTero is captured by a separate signal.
signals.note: Digital-readiness signal, open-ecosystem only. A brand-agnostic scanner exports open STL files and slots directly into Angel's workflow with no equipment change. iTero is deliberately excluded — it is Align Technology's scanner and is scored separately as friction. No penalty for absence (not_found is neutral).

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice's website name the iTero intraoral scanner as equipment they use?
signals.note: FRICTION. iTero is Align Technology's proprietary scanner — an iTero-only practice has its digital workflow built around Invisalign, the strongest practical lock-in short of a Diamond badge. A confirmed 'yes' subtracts from fit. A practice naming both iTero and an open scanner nets positive (S-OA-002 + this), correctly reading as multi-path.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice's website explicitly mention patient financing, payment plans, or third-party financing partners such as CareCredit, LendingClub, or similar?
signals.note: Value-tier consult-capture signal. Financing visibility indicates the practice converts cost-sensitive patients — Angel's margin argument lands here.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice explicitly list adult orthodontic treatment or show messaging directed at adult patients seeking braces or aligners?
signals.note: Adult aligner market. The majority of Angel's addressable consult volume is adult cash-pay.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this appear to be an independently owned orthodontic practice — not part of a DSO, not hospital-owned, and not affiliated with a dental school?
signals.note: THE WEDGE. Independence means direct budget authority, faster device adoption, and no committee purchasing layer.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice display an Invisalign Diamond, Diamond Plus, or Top 1% provider badge or tier designation, AND does the site NOT list any clear aligner brand other than Invisalign (such as ClearCorrect, Spark, SureSmile, or similar)? Mark 'yes' only when BOTH conditions are true: high-tier badge present AND no competing aligner brands mentioned.
signals.note: HARD EXCLUSION — high-tier Align lock with no multi-brand evidence. A Diamond/Diamond Plus/Top 1% practice that exclusively lists Invisalign is commercially unreachable: deep volume incentives, iTero workflow dependency, and staff trained on a single system. Not worth the conversion attempt. If the practice also lists competing brands alongside the badge, S-OA-020 fires instead and routes to Contender.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice display an Invisalign Diamond, Diamond Plus, or Top 1% provider badge or tier designation AND also explicitly list at least one clear aligner brand other than Invisalign — such as ClearCorrect, Spark, SureSmile, or similar — as an offered treatment? Mark 'yes' only when BOTH conditions are true: high-tier badge present AND at least one competing aligner brand also listed.
signals.note: ROUTING FLAG — high-tier Align practice that has already opened its aligner stack. Multi-brand openness is confirmed; the pitch shifts to lab-fee delta on cost-sensitive consults rather than a full platform switch. Capped at Contender because the deep Align relationship still creates switching resistance for core cases. Mutually exclusive with S-OA-006: exactly one fires when a high-tier badge is present.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice's website or any named affiliation identify it as part of a Dental Service Organization (DSO) or corporate dental group?
signals.note: ROUTING FLAG — not a hard exclusion per Decision 2 default. Caps tier at Contender; buying authority sits at corporate level. # CONFIRM: 2026-06-02 — 'buying_authority: corporate' is not a schema field; context is surfaced via tier_cap_reason and sales_angle.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice appear to offer only general dentistry with no orthodontic specialty — no mention of braces, clear aligners, orthodontist on staff, or orthodontic credentials?
signals.note: Hard exclusion for V1 per Decision 3 default. # CONFIRM: 2026-06-02 — Relaxable for future GP channel via 'general_dentist_gate_active' flag in run_config.

signals.prompt_instruction: Is this orthodontic practice part of a hospital outpatient department or is the orthodontist listed as a hospital-employed provider?
signals.note: Hard exclusion. Hospital procurement cycles make device adoption impractical.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice explicitly identify itself as an Angel Aligner or Angelalign Technology provider or partner?
signals.note: Hard exclusion — new-logo acquisition only per Decision 4 default. # CONFIRM: 2026-06-02 — Build upsell motion as a separate ICP tier if needed.

signals.prompt_instruction: Is this practice affiliated with a dental school, orthodontic residency program, or academic medical center?
signals.note: Hard exclusion. Academic procurement and teaching mission make commercial device contracts impractical.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice explicitly list Invisalign as an offered treatment option?
signals.note: Contextual capture. Invisalign users understand digital workflows; sales angle focuses on lab-fee delta (~$1,000 per case savings).

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice list any clear aligner brands other than Invisalign — such as ClearCorrect, Spark, Reveal, or SureSmile — as offered treatments?
signals.note: Multi-brand aligner presence is the lowest-friction adoption signal for Angel. Reinforces the open-scanner signal: Spark, ClearCorrect, and SureSmile all run on open STL workflows, so a practice offering them almost certainly operates a brand-agnostic scanner even when no scanner is named on the site.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice's website explicitly list surgical orthodontics, Class II or Class III correction, extraction cases, or mandibular advancement as case types they treat?
signals.note: Contextual capture. Maps to Angel Pro and A6 Mandibular Advancement product lines.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice explicitly list teen or adolescent orthodontic treatment as a service?
signals.note: Contextual capture. Maps to Angel Aligner teen product line considerations.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice explicitly list Phase 1 orthodontics, interceptive treatment, or early orthodontic care for children?
signals.note: Contextual capture. Maps to Angel KiD and angelHook product lines.

signals.prompt_instruction: Does this practice appear to actively market clear aligners — through dedicated aligner landing pages, before/after aligner case galleries, or aligner-specific call-to-action messaging?
signals.note: Contextual capture. Active aligner marketing signals high patient demand and openness to expanding the aligner product offering.

signals.prompt_instruction: Do patient reviews on this site mention a specific clear aligner brand by name?
signals.note: Contextual capture. Review mentions confirm active aligner use independent of website marketing claims.

demo_accounts.signals_summary: Clear aligners listed (yes, high), 3Shape Trios scanner named (yes, high), CareCredit financing visible (yes, high), adult aligner messaging prominent (yes, high), independent single-location practice (yes, high). No iTero. No Invisalign badge shown. No DSO affiliation.
demo_accounts.sales_angle: Independent Dallas orthodontist offering multiple aligner brands on an open Trios scanner with active adult patient messaging — direct budget authority, zero equipment friction. Lab-fee delta argument is immediately actionable: practice lists ClearCorrect alongside Invisalign, confirming multi-brand openness. Financing visibility signals cost-sensitive patient focus — Angel's value-tier consult-capture story lands directly.
demo_accounts.note: Sample account for field validation. Signals illustrate the target Bullseye profile: four core signals confirmed at high confidence producing fit score ~91.

demo_accounts.practice_name: Coral Gables Orthodontic Group — Miami, FL
demo_accounts.signals_summary: Clear aligners (non-Invisalign): ClearCorrect listed (yes, high — S-OA-001 satisfied), iTero scanner named (yes, high — friction, S-OA-019), financing visible (yes, high), treats adults (yes, high), independent practice (yes, high). Invisalign Diamond Plus badge displayed on homepage. ClearCorrect also listed alongside Invisalign — S-OA-020 fires (high-tier + multi-brand) capping tier at Contender; S-OA-006 does NOT fire because competing brands are present.
demo_accounts.sales_angle: Independent Miami practice with confirmed multi-brand aligner stack (Invisalign + ClearCorrect) and adult patient focus — the lab-fee delta argument is immediately credible because they are already running two aligner systems. The Diamond Plus badge means the Align relationship is real; the pitch is not a switch, it is adding Angel for cost-sensitive and financing-sensitive consults they are currently discounting or losing. iTero scanner is friction but does not block — ClearCorrect runs on open STL, confirming they can already handle non-iTero workflows.
demo_accounts.note: Sample account illustrating S-OA-020 routing (high-tier badge + competing brands present = Contender, not Excluded). If ClearCorrect were absent from this site, S-OA-006 would fire and the account would be Excluded. Compare with the Bullseye sample: same commercial signals, different scanner ecosystem and Align tier.

demo_accounts.practice_name: Phoenix Family Dental & Orthodontics — Phoenix, AZ
demo_accounts.signals_summary: General dentist only: yes — website lists general dentistry, hygiene, fillings, extractions with no orthodontist named, no aligner treatment, no braces mentioned.
demo_accounts.exclusion_reason: general_dentist_only confirmed: practice offers general dentistry only with no orthodontic specialty. Excluded per V1 hard gate (Decision 3 default). If GP channel is approved, re-run with general_dentist_gate_active flag disabled.
demo_accounts.note: Sample account illustrating hard exclusion via exclude_if_yes signal. No LLM enrichment tokens spent; exclusion evaluated at ingestion/structural pass.
